design_philosophy.core_loop: Enter puzzle room → analyze spatial constraints and enemy patterns → manipulate objects and enemies using limited tools (bridge, chests, shots) → clear room of enemies → proceed to next puzzle
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource-constrained spatial problem-solving: limited abilities must be deployed strategically against fixed enemy patterns in confined spaces
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master logic and spatial reasoning to overcome increasingly elaborate puzzle chambers as a lone hero rescuing a kingdom
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rejects action reflexes in favor of pure puzzle logic. Each room is a self-contained logical challenge with no time pressure, emphasizing deliberate planning over execution speed. The game trusts players to understand mechanics through experimentation rather than tutorials.

difficulty.checkpointing: Per-room: each puzzle room is discrete; failure resets only current room
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: game includes optional hidden 'Pro' puzzle rooms for players seeking extreme challenge; core 50-room progression accommodates standard players

progression.upgrade_system: No persistent upgrades. Mechanics introduced gradually: basic movement → bridge creation → projectile attack → chest manipulation → advanced enemy behavior patterns
progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear room sequence. Pro rooms hidden and unlocked through completion or specific discovery criteria
replayability_hooks: Hidden Pro puzzle rooms with very high difficulty
Optional challenge of solving each room with alternative strategies
King Egger boss encounter as final test

world_building_techniques: Progressive environment theming across puzzle rooms
Monster gallery implying ecosystem of kingdom threats
King Egger as final antagonist representing chaos/tyranny

boss_design.philosophy: King Egger serves as final puzzle encounter rather than action boss; victory requires applying mastered mechanics from prior rooms in synthesized form

faction_indicators: Rescue narrative implies player as hero vs. King Egger as tyrannical ruler
Monster diversity (Gol, Rocky, Skull, Medusa, Don Medusa) suggests organized kingdom threat
danger_signals: Progressive difficulty escalation signals mounting challenge from kingdom forces
Pro rooms as ultimate test of player mastery against ruthless challenges
environmental_storytelling: Puzzle room layouts suggest castle/dungeon progression
Monster placement patterns imply intentional dungeon design/guardianship
Resource scarcity (limited shots, bridges) reflects siege/survival scenario

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Adventures of Lolo 2 NES)
